---
title: "Roadmap and status"
---

lims-core is at its first end-to-end milestone: a thin but genuinely
front-to-back vertical slice on a production-shaped compliance core. This page
is the honest summary of what that means. The full, code-checked gap analysis
is the
[completeness review](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core/blob/main/docs/completeness-review.md);
the architecture and build plan are in
[plan.md](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core/blob/main/docs/plan.md).

## Where the line is

lims-core today does one workflow — accession, store, order, result, verify,
sign, audit — and does it the way a modern auditable system should: the audit
trail, e-signatures, chain of custody, and least-privilege enforcement live in
the database as triggers and roles, not in application code a bug could bypass.

It is **not** a production system for running a regulated trial biobank, and
**not** a drop-in replacement for a large commercial LIMS platform. Those
products carry an enormous functional surface — biobanking depth, stability
studies, electronic lab notebooks, dashboards, no-code workflow designers, broad
instrument integration — and lims-core implements a single narrow workflow. The
honest framing is an open, modern nucleus you can grow into that kind of
platform, not a replacement you can install next quarter.

## Working today

Specimen accession, 2D barcode labels, freezer storage with a
one-occupant-per-position constraint, an append-only chain of custody, test
ordering, versioned four-eyes results, re-authenticated e-signatures, a
hash-chained per-study audit trail, grant-based access control, OIDC and local
authentication, and database-enforced immutability — all covered by tests that
run against a real Postgres in CI.

## Designed and reserved, not built

The data model reserves the enum values and lineage structure these will need,
so a future build won't have to migrate an append-only table — but there is no
logic or UI yet:

- Aliquot and derivation trees, and volume/quantity/concentration on samples.
- Consent-withdrawal holds and disposal, propagated from the EDC.
- The analytical module: test specifications and acceptance criteria,
  calculated results, worksheets, QC samples, and certificate-of-analysis
  generation.

## Not started

The real distance to a production LIMS, listed so it can be prioritized rather
than discovered: kits and shipments with custody handoff, bulk accessioning and
plate/rack operations, freezer-map visualization, sample request and
distribution workflows, reagent and lot inventory, instrument registry and
result capture, reporting and dashboards, a configurable workflow state machine,
document/SOP management, and a formal validation package.

## Shortest path to a pilot

If the goal is to run a sponsor's or CRO's biospecimen management, the highest-
leverage next steps, in rough order: aliquot workflow with volume fields; kits
and shipments with custody handoff; bulk accessioning and a freezer-map UI;
consent-withdrawal holds; reporting and manifest exports; then reagent and lot
inventory. Each should land with the discipline the slice already shows — a
schema change plus an ADR, requirement IDs in column comments, and a compliance
test for any new regulated behavior.
